name: demesne-mcp
description: Run heterogeneous AI agents and shell commands in disposable OpenSandbox containers.
long_description:
Demesne is an MCP server that exposes sandboxed command execution backed by [OpenSandbox](https://github.com/alibaba/OpenSandbox).

**Tools:**

- **sandbox_script** — single-shot: run one shell command in a fresh sandbox and tear it down.
- **sandbox_create / sandbox_exec / sandbox_upload / sandbox_download / sandbox_destroy** — persistent sandboxes: spin one up, run multiple commands, move files in and out, then explicitly destroy. Active sandboxes are kept alive by sandbox_exec; idle ones expire after 24h.
- **sandbox_agent** — run an AI coding agent (currently the Claude Code CLI) in a fresh sandbox against a caller-supplied prompt. Outbound HTTPS is restricted to a host-side vendor API proxy, so the agent can reach the model API but nothing else (unless the caller also opts in to 'package-managers' egress).
- **sandbox_research** — like sandbox_agent but for long-running research: no input mounts, unrestricted outbound internet.

All sandboxes are built from a whitelisted image (anaconda, python, node, or — for agents — a demesne-built provider-specific image). Caller-supplied host paths are mounted read-only at /in/<basename>. A writable /out mount is created automatically and its host path is returned so the caller can read produced artifacts; agents also get a writable /workspace as their cwd. Outbound network access is restricted by default — 'package-managers' allows npm/PyPI/conda registries, 'none' denies all egress, 'open' (sandbox_research only) allows everything.

A sandbox runs exactly one vendor proxy, which parses the upstream API's usage events and writes a usage.json to the run's /out directory. The reported cost_usd is the *indicative* spend the run incurred through that proxy — for example, Claude Code OAuth tokens typically authorise against a Claude Console subscription rather than per-request billing, so the user is not charged per request.

allowed_paths.title: Allowed mount paths
allowed_paths.description: Colon-separated list of host directories under which the sandbox_script tool may mount files or directories. Anything outside is rejected.

output_root.title: Output root
output_root.description: Host directory under which per-job /out mounts are created. Defaults to /tmp/demesne/out.

opensandbox_domain.title: OpenSandbox domain
opensandbox_domain.description: host:port of the OpenSandbox lifecycle server (e.g. localhost:8080).

opensandbox_api_key.title: OpenSandbox API key
opensandbox_api_key.description: API key for the OpenSandbox lifecycle server.

claude_code_oauth_token.title: Claude Code OAuth token
claude_code_oauth_token.description: Long-lived OAuth token for Claude Code, produced by `claude setup-token`. Required to run sandbox_agent or sandbox_research; other tools work without it.

tools.name: sandbox_script
tools.description: Run a shell command in a fresh sandbox; returns stdout and the host path of the /out mount

tools.name: sandbox_create
tools.description: Create a persistent sandbox; returns a sandbox handle for sandbox_exec/upload/download/destroy

tools.name: sandbox_exec
tools.description: Run a shell command in an existing sandbox; refreshes the sandbox TTL

tools.name: sandbox_upload
tools.description: Copy a host file into an existing sandbox

tools.name: sandbox_download
tools.description: Copy a file out of an existing sandbox to the host

tools.name: sandbox_destroy
tools.description: Kill an existing sandbox; host output dir is preserved

tools.name: sandbox_agent
tools.description: Run an AI coding agent (currently the Claude Code CLI) in a fresh sandbox; outbound HTTPS is restricted to a host-side per-vendor API proxy

tools.name: sandbox_research
tools.description: Run a long-running research agent in a fresh sandbox with unrestricted outbound internet
